**Business Plan for [Nonprofit Name]**  
**Mission:** To bridge the digital divide in underserved populations and communities within Seattle and King County by providing free, accessible technology training and resources.

---

## **Executive Summary**

[Nonprofit Name] is a non-profit organization dedicated to addressing the digital divide in underserved communities within Seattle and King County. The organization aims to empower individuals and families by providing free technology training, access to digital tools, and ongoing support to ensure equitable access to technology and digital literacy. As the digital landscape continues to evolve, the need for digital literacy and access has never been more critical. [Nonprofit Name] will serve as a vital resource for those who are historically underserved, helping them to participate fully in the digital economy, access essential services, and improve their quality of life.

The organization will operate in a community-based model, offering workshops, one-on-one coaching, and access to technology resources. By focusing on education and empowerment, [Nonprofit Name] will not only address the immediate need for digital skills but also build long-term capacity within the communities it serves.

This business plan outlines the organization’s strategic direction, operational framework, and financial roadmap as it grows from a local initiative to an international model.

---

## **Nonprofit Description**

[Nonprofit Name] is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in [year], with a mission to empower underserved populations in Seattle and King County through technology education and access. The organization is led by a team of experienced educators, technologists, and community advocates who work collaboratively to ensure that every individual has the opportunity to thrive in a digital world.

The organization is based in [location], and its primary office is located at [address]. It operates in partnership with local community centers, schools, libraries, and nonprofits to provide services in a variety of settings. [Nonprofit Name] is committed to inclusivity, equity, and accessibility, ensuring that all individuals, regardless of background or socioeconomic status, have equal access to the resources and support they need.

---

## **Need Analysis**

The digital divide in Seattle and King County is a pressing issue that affects a significant portion of the population. According to recent data from the **Seattle City Council** and the **King County Department of Information Technology**, over 20% of residents in low-income households lack access to high-speed internet, and more than 30% of adults in the region lack basic digital literacy skills.
